reasoning: The question asks if the number of 'Violence against civilians' events in Nigeria over a specific 30-day window (Feb 20 - Mar 21, 2026) will exceed the 30-day average of the preceding 360 days (which serves as a lagging 6-month average). Background searches indicate that violence in Nigeria, including attacks by the JAS faction and other groups, has been persistently high and generally trending upward according to 2025/2026 ACLED and HRW reports. An upward long-term trend makes the most recent month structurally more likely to exceed a 360-day lagging baseline. While there is considerable month-to-month volatility and some dry-season vs rainy-season effects (which can elevate some types of violence while depressing others), the combination of a high baseline of violence and escalating recent conflict reports pushes the probability favorably above 50%. I estimate this at roughly 68% given structural drift and baseline volatility.
